name: Alella and Turó d'en Galzeran Loop
name_original: De Alella a Els Ginestells — recorrido circular
description: Hike the moderate 7.9-mile (12.7 km) From Alella to Els Ginestells circular route, featuring vineyard views and panoramic summit vistas.
region_intro: Hiking around Alella offers diverse natural features within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral and Parc de la Serralada de Marina. The region is characterized by gently rolling landscapes, vineyards, and mountainous terrain. Its proximity to the Mediterranean Sea provides coastal views from many trails and elevated points. Best hiking trails around Alella The most popular hiking route is GR 92: Turó d'en Galzeran Loop, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This easy route features a fire watchtower and offers extensive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alella and Turó d'en Galzeran Loop, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This trail winds through hilly landscapes and vineyards, providing panoramic coastal views. Local hikers also love the Mirador de la Cornisa, Sagrat Cor y refugio de la Ferreria del Vedat — circular, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Alella is defined by vineyards, Mediterranean coastal views, and the varied terrain of the Parc de la Serralada Litoral. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging hikes. The routes in Alella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Alella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alella?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Alella, offering a variety of experiences. You'll find options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging hikes, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for families or beginners in Alella?
A: Yes, Alella offers several easy routes perfect for families or those new to hiking. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324929393/gr-92-turo-d-en-galzeran-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 92: Turó d'en Galzeran Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.3 km trail that takes less than two hours to complete and features extensive views from a fire watchtower.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes in Alella?
A: Hiking in Alella offers stunning panoramic views. Many trails provide vistas of the Mediterranean Sea, the surrounding vineyards, and the hilly landscapes of the Parc de la Serralada Litoral. From elevated points like Turó de Montcada or La Coscollada, you can often see as far as Barcelona, Montserrat, and even the Pyrenees on clear days.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alella?
A: Yes, many routes in Alella are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350830710/mirador-de-la-cornisa-sagrat-cor-y-refugio-de-la-ferreria-del-vedat-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Cornisa, Sagrat Cor y refugio de la Ferreria del Vedat — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 12.5 km long and takes you through the Parc de la Serralada Litoral.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I discover while hiking in Alella?
A: Alella's trails often lead to interesting historical and cultural sites. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar Iberian Settlement&lt;/a&gt;, an important fortified town, or encounter megalithic monuments like the Can Gurri dolmen. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; also offers historical context along with its views.

Q: Are there any viewpoints worth visiting on the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its spectacular viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; and Turó d'en Galzeran are popular spots for extensive views. The Mirador de Nou Pins, located within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, is another prime location for breathtaking panoramas of Alella and the coastline.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alella?
A: The hiking trails in Alella are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful vineyard landscapes, and the rewarding coastal and mountain views.

Q: Can I hike with my dog on the trails around Alella?
A: Many trails in the Parc de la Serralada Litoral and Parc de la Serralada de Marina are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and respect local regulations, especially in protected areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alella?
A: Alella is suitable for hiking year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for longer excursions. Winter can also be excellent, with crisp air and clear views. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access hiking trails in Alella?
A: Alella is accessible by public transport from Barcelona, making it possible to reach the starting points of various trails without a car. Local bus services connect the town to nearby areas and can often drop you close to trailheads within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Alella?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Alella, particularly near popular trailheads or within the town itself. Many routes, especially those starting from the town, offer convenient access to parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details for the most suitable parking options.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through vineyards or offer winery visits?
A: Yes, Alella is renowned for its vineyards, and many hiking trails wind through these scenic landscapes. Wineries like Alta Alella are often nestled in elevated locations, making them accessible by foot and offering opportunities for visits and tastings after your hike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19206089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyard loop from Alella&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a route that immerses you in the wine region.
tips: Large area where you can take fantastic photographs of the Mediterranean coast and the province of Barcelona, extending towards Mataró.

The Can Gurri dolmen, also known as the Collet de Can Gurri dolmen or Gurri fountain dolmen, is a megalithic monument discovered in 1952. Some of the galleries and vertical slabs of the chamber are preserved.

Don't forget to go down this beautiful path, a snake-shaped path, one of the few that I have seen in more than 70 routes already highlighted, with unique views where the vineyards and stone pines accompany you. The area is like a movie 🐶🌲🥾
During the route, almost reaching its end, you will find next to you, the highest point of the route, with beautiful views, the stones create a small base where only one person can be, it is curious.

faqs.question: How challenging is the From Alella to Els Ginestells — circular route?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate hike, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. While most paths are accessible, some sections might be uneven or slightly challenging underfoot. It's suitable for individuals who can comfortably hike for up to 5 hours with moderate elevation changes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 12.7 kilometers (7.9 miles) long and generally takes about 4 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, the route starts and ends in Alella. It's advisable to look for public parking areas within Alella, especially near the town center, which typically serves as a convenient starting point for local trails.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Alella is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services from nearby towns and Barcelona. Check local bus schedules for routes that stop in Alella, allowing you to reach the starting point of the hike.

faqs.question: Is the From Alella to Els Ginestells — circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally dog-friendly. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through vineyards, near livestock, or in areas where wildlife might be present. Remember to carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for hiking this route. The temperatures are milder, and the scenery, particularly the vineyards, is often at its most beautiful. Summer can be very hot, so if hiking then, start early to avoid the midday sun. Winter offers cooler, crisp air but can be damp.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a variety of sights. You'll encounter the View of Barcelona from Mirador de Sant Mateu, the historical Dolmen de Can Gurri, and sections of the Maresme Vineyards Trail. You'll also reach the View from Els Ginestells Summit, offering panoramic vistas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Montgat-Tordera, GR 92 BTT, Tiana-Teià, and De la Tordera al Llobregat. Variant Palaudaries - Alella trails, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the From Alella to Els Ginestells — circular route, as it is located within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina, which is freely accessible to the public. However, always respect park regulations regarding litter, noise, and fire prevention.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and 4-hour duration, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear (with good grip for uneven sections), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for the weather. A map or GPS device is also advisable.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with young children?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, 12.7 km length, and approximately 490 meters of elevation gain, this route might be challenging for very young children or those not accustomed to hiking. It's better suited for families with older children or teenagers who have good fitness and hiking experience.

waypoints.name: View of Barcelona from Mirador de Sant Mateu
